explanation: This LTL property ensures that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state ('state_is_TRANSITION') and both the system is supported ('supported') and sensor data is good ('good'), the autopilot will transition to the NOMINAL state ('state_is_NOMINAL') in the next time step.

The LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition to the NOMINAL state in the next state. This is captured by the implication: (supported & good) -> X (state_is_NOMINAL).

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2) and the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp1), then in the next state the autopilot will be in the NOMINAL state (bool_exp3).

The LTL property asserts that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state after the REQUEST mode. This captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL when the system is supported and sensor data is good, by ensuring that the transition to NOMINAL state occurs eventually after the transition mode has been activated.

The LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp1), then in the next state (X), the autopilot will transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state (bool_exp3).

It uses the implication operator to express the relationship between the condition (supported and good) and the consequence (transition to NOMINAL in the next state).

It asserts that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state after being in the TRANSITION state.

The property is expressed using a combination of the always operator (G) and the next operator (X). The always operator ensures that the condition holds in all future states. The boolean expressions 'bool_exp2' and 'bool_exp3' capture the system being supported and the sensor data being good, respectively, while 'bool_exp3' captures the transition to the NOMINAL state.

The chosen template, G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)), ensures that this transition behavior occurs globally in all reachable states of the system.

The boolean expressions are constructed using the atomic propositions provided, where "supported" represents the system being healthy, "good" represents the sensor data being valid, and "state_is_TRANSITION" and "state_is_NOMINAL" represent the autopilot's current state.

The LTL formula ensures that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition to the NOMINAL state from the TRANSITION state.  The formula uses the "G" operator to indicate that this property must hold globally (at all times).  The "->" operator represents implication, meaning that if the condition on the left-hand side is true, then the condition on the right-hand side must also be true. The condition on the left-hand side is a conjunction of the atomic propositions "supported" and "good", indicating that the system is supported and the sensor data is good. The condition on the right-hand side is a conjunction of the atomic propositions "!state_is_TRANSITION" and "state_is_NOMINAL", indicating that the autopilot is no longer in the TRANSITION state and has transitioned to the NOMINAL state.

This LTL property ensures that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state within N_DURATION time steps. It uses the implication operator (->) to express this relationship.  The first part of the implication checks if the system is supported and the sensor data is good, represented by the boolean expression "(supported & good)".  The second part of the implication checks if the autopilot transitions from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state within N_DURATION time steps.  This is achieved by using the "F[0,N_DURATION]" operator to check if the state becomes NOMINAL within N_DURATION time steps, and then checking if the state was previously in the TRANSITION state using the boolean expression "state_is_TRANSITION".  The "G" operator ensures that this implication holds in all future states.

If this condition holds, then the property requires that eventually the autopilot transitions to the NOMINAL state (bool_exp3). This is captured using the "F" (eventually) operator.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state. The formula uses the G operator to express that this requirement holds globally, meaning it must be true in all reachable states. The formula also uses the F operator to express that the transition to the NOMINAL state will eventually occur. The boolean expression `(supported & good)` ensures that the system is supported and sensor data is good before the transition can occur. The boolean expression `(state_is_TRANSITION & !state_is_NOMINAL)` ensures that the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state before the transition to the NOMINAL state. The N_DURATION is set to 1, meaning that the transition is expected to occur within a single time step.
